ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Amended Petition For Letters Of Administration With Will Annexed By Lincoln Lucero.
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Motion To Vacate The Order Appointing Keith Lucero Administrator With Will Annexed
Off Calendar Apr-27-2010 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Russell Stanaland for Lincoln Lucero. John Harbour for and with Keith Lucero.

Mr. Stanaland stated that Keith Lucero is not the appropriate party to be appointed administrator, that he had mis-appropriated funds, and that there was an altercation because of the mis-appropriation. Mr. Harbour argued that there is a personal injury action pending to ascertain what happened between the brothers, that Mr. Emmers was negligently carrying out his duties, that Keith has been moving forward, that even if Lincoln is appointed he won't be objective, and that if Keith is not appointed he is requesting that the Public Administrator be appointed. Mr. Stanaland argued that Keith Lucero took funds from the Estate, that Lincoln is bondable, and that Keith would be bonded but the funds would have to be in a blocked account. Mr. Harbour responded as set forth on the record. The matters were then taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-25-2010 Continued To Mar-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Harbour and Alexis Donohoe for Keith Lucero. Russell Stanaland for Lincoln Lucero.

The Court stated that there were two competing petitions and that there was the issue of whether or not Mr. Emmer has the lost will. Mr. Stanaland and Mr. Harbour both stated that they had not spoken with Mr. Emmer. The Court issued an Order to Show Cause to Robert Emmer ordering him to deliver the original will of Marlene S. Wills or appear and show cause why he cannot produce the original will. The return date is February 8, 2010. The Court inquired whether Keith Lucero and Lincoln Lucero are bondable. Mr. Harbour stated that his client is still seeking employment and he hopes that he is bondable. Mr. Stanaland confirmed that his client is bondable. The Court stated that the procedural issues regarding Keith are cured but that there are still issues with Lincoln. Mr. Stanaland stated that notice was sent and that he had forgotten to check one box regarding the IAEA. The Court stated that it appeared that there was missing information on the proof, that Mr. Stanaland would need to file his own proof of publication, cure the authority issue, and submit a proposed order. The Court continued the competing petitions to March 1, 2010. The Court inquired about the likelihood of one of the brothers stepping aside if it turns out that they both are bondable. Mr. Stanaland stated that if Keith can produce full bond then they will consider stepping aside. The Court stated that full bond would be required. Ms. Donoghoe stated that they would be looking into bonding Keith today. The Court ordered Counsel to inform the Court by the close of business on February 23, 2010 if Keith Lucero is bondable. In addition, the Court noted that if Counsel cannot agree then it will make the determination as to who will serve.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli
